case_title: LUCERO, MARY ELLEN vs. AYERS, DAVID MIKE et al

docket_entries.description: ORDER ALLOWING WITHDRAWAL OF COUNSEL FILED

PERSONAL INJURY/PROPERTY DAMAGE - VEHICLE RELATED, COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF LUCERO, MARY ELLEN AS TO DEFENDANT AYERS, DAVID MIKE DOES 1 TO 5, INCLUSIVE SUMMONS ISSUED STATUS CONFERENCE SCHEDULED FOR          AUG-23-1995 PROOF OF SERVICE DUE ON                  JUN-26-1995 STATUS CONFERENCE STATEMENT DUE ON       AUG-03-1995
MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF LUCERO, MARY ELLEN HEARING SET FOR JUN-12-1995 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 02
LAW AND MOTION, DEPT 02, MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD, CALENDAR OF JUN-12-1995, GRANTED PURSUANT TO WRITTEN ORDER PLAINTIFF COUNSEL TO PREPARE ORDER WITH ADMONITION.
SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY: PRO PER SUBSTITUTED FOR CASTRO, RAMIRO RODRIGUEZ AS ATTORNEY FOR LUCERO, MARY ELLEN

STATUS CONFERENCE CALENDAR OF AUG-23-1995, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE FOR FAILURE TO APPEAR AT STATUS CONFERENCE ON AUG-23-1995, TO SERVE DEFENDANT AND FILE PROOF OF SERVICE WITHIN 60 DAYS OF FILING OF COMPLAINT, TO FILE A STATUS CONFERENCE STATEMENT WITHIN 20 CALENDAR DAYS OF STATUS CONFERENCE DATE (LOCAL RULE 303E4), AND WHY THE COURT SHOULD NOT DISMISS CASE OR TAKE OTHER APPROPRIATE ACTION. HEARING SET FOR SEP-22-1995 AT 02:00 PM IN DEPT 04 NOTICE SENT BY COURT

OSC CALENDAR OF SEP-22-1995, SANCTIONS ORDERED IN THE AMOUNT OF $50.00 FOR FAILURE TO APPEAR AT ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE HEARING ON SEP-22-1995, TO SERVE DEFENDANT AND FILE PROOF OF SERVICE WITHIN 60 DAYS OF FILING OF COMPLAINT, TO FILE A STATUS CONFERENCE STATEMENT WITHIN 20 CALENDAR DAYS OF STATUS CONFERENCE DATE (LOCAL RULE 303E4), 2ND OSC: IF NO GOOD CAUSE IS SHOWN COURT WILL DISMISS CASE., PAYABLE ON OR BEFORE OCT-15-1995 CONTINUED FROM SEP-22-1995 TO OCT-23-1995 AT 02:00 PM NOTICE SENT BY COURT
OSC CALENDAR OF OCT-23-1995, DISMISSAL PURSUANT TO SECTIONS 581(D) AND 581(M) OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE AND CALIFORNIA CASUALTY INDEMNITY CO. VS SUPERIOR COURT (1995) 36 CAL APP 4TH 678
